narrative: OMX30 enters Friday with structural bullish technicals—MACD crossover above signal, positioned +8.4% above the 200-day MA, and RSI at 59.78 neutral—but macro headwinds and weak ADX (12.3) sharply reduce conviction. Global equity markets closed modestly red: DAX -0.53%, NASDAQ -0.48%, Nikkei -1.23%, creating a -0.393% weighted correlation drag. US 10Y yields spiked +68bp to 4.603%, a positive for Swedish bank margins but a headwind for tech valuations and real-estate refinancing. Brent oil surged +1.78%, pressuring industrials (28% of index). The combination of conflicting signals—technical strength versus macro weakness and low momentum trend-following strength (ADX <18)—warrants a cautious NEUTRAL stance with modest upside bias only if USD/SEK stabilization holds.
morningBrief: OMX30 likely opens flat to slightly positive, supported by MACD bullish crossover and +2.4% above the 50-MA. However, expect choppy trade given global correlation headwind (-0.393%) and weak ADX momentum (12.3). Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken, Swedbank) should outperform on the 68bp US yield spike—wider NIM tailwind—but industrials (Atlas Copco, Volvo, Sandvik) face pressure from +1.78% oil and input-cost concern. Tech/telecom (Ericsson, Hexagon) remain under NASDAQ sentiment overhang (-0.48%). Key support at 3085 (lower Bollinger band); resistance at 3180 (50-MA). Watch US Jobless Claims (14:30 CET) for volatility catalyst. Risk scenario: continued risk-off could break 3085 and trigger -1.5% downside. Position defensively; avoid large directional bets until ADX strengthens above 18.
drivers: Weak global correlation signal (-0.393%) offsetting OMX30 bull structure
ADX at 12.3 (below 18 threshold) reduces conviction; apply -30% magnitude discount
US yields +68bp and VIX compression favor banks, but oil +1.78% pressures industrials
OMX30 bull bias (+8.4% vs 200-MA) conflicts with risk-off sentiment across DAX, CAC, NASDAQ

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +68bp to 4.603% directly widens Swedish bank NIM expectations and supports net interest margin growth.
Brent oil +1.78% to 106.9 raises input and transportation costs; headwind to margins and guidance.
NASDAQ -0.48% and broader risk-off sentiment create valuation pressure; tech sentiment remains fragile.
Copper +0.09% and commodities firm; gold -0.28% is modest headwind but oil strength supports energy/metals themes.
Higher US yields (+68bp) flow through to Nordic refinancing costs and property discount rates; cap rates compress valuations.

watchList.reason: US 10Y yield spike to 4.603% is a direct positive for NIM; likely to outperform on yield momentum.
Copper +0.09% is marginal; gold -0.28% is a mild drag. Positioning neutral until clearer commodity trend.
NASDAQ -0.48% and global risk-off sentiment remain headwinds; wait for ADX strengthening or NASDAQ stabilization.
USD/SEK -0.30% slightly weakens export tail; oil +1.78% pressures auto costs. Conflicting signals—hold on sideline.

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims (14:30 CET) is primary catalyst. No major Swedish economic data released today. Monitor for US macro surprises that could re-rate yields and global risk sentiment.
topRisk: Global risk-off acceleration: if DAX, CAC, or NASDAQ break key supports, OMX30 bull structure breaks below 3085 and could tumble -1.5% to -2.0%. Trade war escalation or central-bank hawkish surprise would trigger sharp VIX spike and equity rotation.
technicalSummary: MACD bullish crossover above signal (early momentum)
ADX 12.3 WEAK (below 18 threshold)—reduce conviction by 30%; trend-following strength is LOW
Stochastic K=65 D=51.3 neutral; no mean-reversion signal yet
Bollinger %B=0.76 upper half; width 4.61% indicates moderate volatility
52-week range 87% of scale; -3.3% from high (room to run on upside, but proximity to range top suggests caution)
